sourceMovie.title: Birth of the Dragon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Birth of the Dragon works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Karate Kid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Birth of the Dragon clicked for you, The Karate Kid (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bloodsport
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Birth of the Dragon clicked for you, Bloodsport (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Birth of the Dragon?
faq.answer: Try The Karate Kid, Bloodsport, Karate Kid: Legends—each shares DNA with Birth of the Dragon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Birth of the Dragon?
faq.answer: Birth of the Dragon (2017) is best described as Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Birth of the Dragon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
